articleSummary: The article argues that while Docker is powerful and flexible, its early OSX implementation via boot2docker was slow and crash-prone, whereas modern solutions like Docker Desktop (proprietary) or Rancher Desktop (open source) offer a better experience. It provides technical guidance on optimizing a Docker setup, including checking security with `nmap`, using local DNS caching, and selecting the `overlay2` storage driver. The tone is part tutorial, part historical analysis, and the article is framed as a preserved 2015-era snapshot, updated with 2024 notes. The intended audience is developers running Docker on macOS (or Windows) who want performance advice or an overview of the ecosystem’s evolution.
